items.title: [Workspace]: Add a local-only installation mode so agents can use Agentic Workspace in repositories they do not own without requiring checked-in adoption
[Workspace]: Strip away visible product residue that is no longer earning its keep so the system becomes easier to enter, recover, and trust without losing real leverage
[Workspace]: Design for graceful partial compliance so generic agents still get value from the package, and bypassing it becomes visible and harder to trust
[Workspace]: Make core package surfaces cheaper for generic agents to consume so using the package is easier than broad repo scavenging or private reinvention
[Workspace]: Make the default entry path easier for generic agents to discover and follow so startup compliance does not depend on deep product awareness
[Workspace]: Make core package surfaces easier for generic agents to use so package startup and state feel cheaper than ad hoc repo reading
[Review]: Surface lower-trust signals when package use was likely bypassed so review and closure do not silently assume disciplined package usage
[Workspace]: Reopen declarative portability so stable lifecycle and runtime truth stops depending on large Python-owned implementations
[Review]: Reopen delegated-run review so mixed-agent work can show what actually changed without broad diff reconstruction
[Planning]: Reopen execplan modernization so machine-readable planning state becomes canonical and human views become derived
[Planning]: Surface planning-surface drift immediately at resume/startup so interrupted-work recovery does not waste effort rediscovering template, checker, or contract mismatches

[Memory]: Turn unlogged recurring friction into durable improvement pressure so agents do not silently notice the same package failures without collecting evidence or pushing them toward product improvement
[Planning]: Add an explicit planning-memory handshake so agents are prompted to pull what they may need before work and preserve what later work may need after finishing
[Planning]: Prompt pre-work memory pull explicitly so agents ask what they may need to know, and about which area, before they begin bounded execution
[Planning]: Prompt post-work posterity capture explicitly so agents ask what should be preserved for later recovery, follow-on work, or future improvement pressure after finishing
[Planning]: Add an explicit planning↔config handshake so bounded work pulls material config before execution and checks config compliance after finishing
[Memory]: Add an explicit memory↔config handshake so memory creation asks how current config wants signals, notes, promotion, and cleanup to be treated
[Workspace]: Surface dangling larger intent and lower-trust closeout signals without assuming any external planning system
[Workspace]: Make the default entry path easier for generic agents to discover and follow so startup compliance does not depend on deep product awareness
[Workspace]: Formalize modules and repo-custom workflow extensions in the orchestrator so planning can consume them when relevant
[Workspace]: Build a repo-owned, vendor-independent, structured agent configuration system that planning, memory, and other behavior modules can plug into
[Workspace]: Define the structured repo-owned agent configuration model, authority map, and module attachment points
[Workspace]: Design selective loading, compact queries, and lazy discovery for structured repo-owned agent configuration
[Workspace]: Make AGENTS.md, llms.txt, and other prose entry surfaces compatibility adapters over structured agent config rather than the primary authority
[Workspace]: Make system intent shape slice implementation tradeoffs, review, and aligned follow-on work rather than acting only as high-level directional prose
[Workspace]: Slim the shipped root AGENTS entrypoint so installs converge on the thin startup contract
[Workspace]: Add a package-owned mirrored system-intent contract with source declaration and sync
Improve takeover robustness with preflight-first workflow gates
[Workspace]: Replace markdown-first workflow contracts with a stricter, composable workflow definition format that stays flexible inside each component step
[Planning]: Take relevant system intent into explicit account when shaping slices and validating intent satisfaction for broader lanes
[Planning]: Add a multi-level structured reference model so execplans and higher-level planning surfaces can point explicitly to relevant issues, files, ranges, URLs, images, and other artifacts
[Workspace]: Replace generated compatibility artefacts with minimal static routing tables so canonical workflows stay discoverable without creating extra mutable surfaces
[Planning]: Define proportional retention for planning review artifacts so review history stays useful without becoming a parallel archive
[Workspace]: Resolve portable execplan semantics against local execution posture so the current environment can optimize quality, cost, and delegation behavior
[Workspace]: Add a first preflight/runtime-resolution slice that turns portable work semantics plus local config into actionable delegation and handoff guidance

[Workspace]: Add a local-only agent integration area for vendor/runtime-specific shortcuts, bridge files, and shims that help agents reach canonical repo-native outcomes without becoming shared workflow state
[Workspace]: Define the first local integration root, vendor subfolder convention, and boundary rules for local-only agent/runtime bridges
[Workspace]: Add a compact handoff/prompt-generation slice for strong general-purpose reasoning so high-judgment questions can be escalated cheaply without moving the whole tranche
[Workspace]: Add preparatory refinement templates for strong general-purpose reasoning so issues and candidate work can be clarified before coding-agent ingestion
[Workspace]: Delegate system-intent extraction to agents
Review friction: reduce competing first-contact surfaces
Direction / proposal: diff-path-based proof selector command
Review friction: adapter vs canonical surface ambiguity
Direction / proposal: minimum-safe-read startup command
Review friction: package boundary confusion (source vs payload vs installed)
Direction / proposal: cheap-implementer mode for bounded agents
Direction / proposal: extract CLI command and option manifests
Direction / proposal: extract module registry metadata from Python
Direction / proposal: extract CLI policy payloads (defaults surfaces)
Direction / proposal: extract setup findings and preflight policy
Direction / proposal: extract workspace surface and path manifests
[Draft][Workspace]: Define implementation-independent operation contracts
[Draft][Workspace]: Add black-box conformance contracts for generated tools
[Draft][Workspace]: Distill durable lane learning before archiving execplans
[Workspace]: Complete Python-consumable contract extraction
[Workspace]: Audit remaining Python-owned extractable contract content
[Workspace]: Extract input/output and result schemas for Python consumption
[Workspace]: Extract static data, text, and rendering templates from Python
[Workspace]: Complete operation-contract coverage for every CLI command
[Workspace]: Wire Python loaders and schema-derived models for extracted contracts
[Workspace]: Add extraction parity checks and Python-consumed contract conformance
[Workspace]: Classify remaining Python-owned procedural logic after extraction
[Review]: Make promote-to-plan support compact TOML active items
